questions.q: In which present-day Indian state is the Hoysaleswara Temple located?
questions.explanation: The Hoysaleswara Temple stands at Halebidu in the Hassan district of Karnataka.

questions.q: The Hoysaleswara Temple is dedicated to which Hindu deity?
questions.explanation: It is a Shaiva temple dedicated to Lord Shiva, worshipped here as Hoysaleswara.

questions.q: In which town is the Hoysaleswara Temple situated?
questions.explanation: The temple is located in Halebidu, the former Hoysala capital once known as Dwarasamudra.

questions.q: Which ruling dynasty built the Hoysaleswara Temple?
questions.explanation: The temple was built by the Hoysala dynasty, after whom it is named.

questions.q: In which century was the Hoysaleswara Temple constructed?
questions.explanation: Construction began in the 12th century during the reign of the Hoysala kings.

questions.q: What is a distinctive feature of the Hoysaleswara Temple's design?
options: It is a twin temple with two main shrines
It is a single circular tower
It is a cave cut into rock
questions.explanation: It is a twin temple, housing two linked shrines side by side, each with its own Shiva linga.

questions.q: What is the Hoysaleswara Temple most famous for?
options: Intricately carved stone friezes and sculptures
questions.explanation: The temple is celebrated for its dense, finely detailed sculptural friezes covering its walls.

questions.q: Which large guardian animal is depicted in famous monolithic statues at the temple?
questions.explanation: Two large Nandi (bull) statues, the mount of Shiva, face the temple's twin shrines.

questions.q: Halebidu, where the temple stands, served as what during the Hoysala period?
options: A military fort with no temples
questions.explanation: Halebidu, then called Dwarasamudra, was the capital of the Hoysala kingdom.

questions.q: The Hoysaleswara Temple is an example of which broad architectural tradition?
questions.explanation: It is a prime example of the distinctive Hoysala style of temple architecture.

questions.q: What stone was primarily used to carve the Hoysaleswara Temple?
questions.explanation: Hoysala temples were carved from soft chlorite schist, or soapstone, which allowed extremely fine detail.

questions.q: During whose reign was construction of the Hoysaleswara Temple begun?
questions.explanation: The temple was begun during the reign of the Hoysala king Vishnuvardhana in the 12th century.

questions.q: By what earlier name was Halebidu known when it was the Hoysala capital?
questions.explanation: Halebidu was historically known as Dwarasamudra (also Dorasamudra), the Hoysala capital.

questions.q: The name 'Halebidu' is commonly understood to mean what in Kannada?
questions.explanation: 'Halebidu' translates roughly as 'old capital' or 'ruined city', reflecting its decline after invasions.

questions.q: The two shrines of the twin temple are dedicated to Shiva under which two names?
questions.explanation: The shrines are named Hoysaleswara and Shantaleswara, the latter associated with Queen Shantala Devi.

questions.q: Hoysala temples like Hoysaleswara typically stand on what kind of base platform?
options: A raised star-shaped (stellate) platform called jagati
questions.explanation: Hoysala temples are raised on a jagati, a wide platform that often follows a star-shaped plan.

questions.q: What kind of subjects fill the horizontal bands of friezes wrapping the temple walls?
options: Rows of elephants, lions, horsemen, and scenes from epics
questions.explanation: Continuous friezes depict elephants, lions, horsemen, and narrative scenes from Hindu epics and mythology.

questions.q: Roughly how many richly carved smaller sculptures decorate the Hoysaleswara Temple?
questions.explanation: The temple bears thousands of detailed sculptures, making its walls among the most densely carved in India.

questions.q: Which Hoysala-style architectural feature is the temple's tower or superstructure called?
questions.explanation: The shrine's superstructure is a shikhara or vimana, though the towers of Hoysaleswara are now largely missing.

questions.q: The Hoysala capital at Halebidu was sacked in the 14th century by armies of which sultanate?
questions.explanation: Dwarasamudra was plundered by the Delhi Sultanate armies in the early 14th century, leading to the city's ruin.

questions.q: Lathe-turned, bell-shaped columns are a hallmark of which architectural tradition seen in the temple's mandapa?
questions.explanation: Polished, lathe-turned pillars in the pillared hall are a signature element of Hoysala craftsmanship.

questions.q: Hoysala temples such as Hoysaleswara are closely associated with which famous nearby temple at Belur?
questions.explanation: The Chennakeshava Temple at Belur is a sister Hoysala monument built in the same era and style.

questions.q: Which Hoysala official and general is traditionally credited with commissioning the Hoysaleswara Temple?
questions.explanation: Inscriptions credit Ketamalla, an officer in Vishnuvardhana's court, with sponsoring the temple around 1121 CE.

questions.q: Queen Shantala Devi, linked to one of the twin shrines, was the consort of which Hoysala king?
questions.explanation: Shantala Devi was the chief queen of King Vishnuvardhana, and the Shantaleswara shrine honors her.

questions.q: What status do Hoysaleswara and other Hoysala temples hold with UNESCO?
options: A declared World Heritage Site since 2023
questions.explanation: The 'Sacred Ensembles of the Hoysalas', including Halebidu, were inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2023.

questions.q: A famous detailed sculpture at the temple depicts Shiva slaying which demon?
questions.explanation: One celebrated panel shows Shiva as Gajasurasamhara, dancing on the slain elephant-demon Gajasura.

questions.q: The Hoysaleswara Temple's plan is often described as which shape?
questions.explanation: Hoysala temples use a stellate (star-shaped) plan formed by rotating squares, multiplying the carved wall surface.

questions.q: Why are the soapstone friezes of Hoysaleswara so exceptionally detailed compared to many other temples?
options: The stone was painted not carved
Soapstone is soft when quarried and hardens with exposure, allowing fine carving
questions.explanation: Chlorite schist is relatively soft when freshly quarried, letting artisans carve minute detail before it hardened over time.

questions.q: Which body administers and protects the Hoysaleswara Temple as a monument of national importance?
questions.explanation: The temple is a protected monument under the care of the Archaeological Survey of India.

questions.q: Hoysala friezes uniquely sometimes include the signatures or names of which group?
options: The sculptors and artisans who carved them
questions.explanation: Many Hoysala carvings bear the inscribed names of the individual sculptors, a rare practice celebrating the artists.
